metadata.title: CROP: Certifying Robust Policies for Reinforcement Learning through Functional Smoothing

sections.title: Background & Motivation
content: Testing and improving the robustness of safety-critical applications before their massive deployment to real-world is of great importance.
Robustness certification for a given learning algorithm is needed to end the repeated game between the attackers and defenders.

sections.title: Threat Model
content: Testing-time evasion attack on agent's observation. Adversary can apply l2-bounded perturbations.

figures.description: Reinforcement learning threat model showing adversary perturbing state observations during testing phase.

sections.title: Certification Goal
content: Provide test-time certification for the performance of the trained agent in the perturbed environment.
1. Providing criteria for robustness certification.
2. Proposing certification strategies corresponding to different certification criteria.

sections.title: Certification Criteria
content: Criterion 1 (state-level):Per-state action stability. Maximum Perturbation Magnitude ε.
Criterion 2 (trajectory-level):Cumulative reward lower bound. Lower bound of perturbed cumulative reward J.

sections.title: CROP Framework
content: Per-State Action Certification (CROP-LoAct):Local randomized smoothing on states. Action-value functional smoothing.
Lipschitz continuity of Q w.r.t. the state input.
Lower bound of ε calculation based on smoothed policy.

sections.title: Cumulative Reward Certification
content: CROP-GRe:Global smoothing on trajectories.
Expectation bound via mean smoothing.
Percentile bound via percentile smoothing.

sections.title: CROP-LoRe: Local smoothing + adaptive search
content: Sequence of certified radii for actions and adaptive search algorithm.
1. Trajectory exploration and expansion: obtain the lower bound for ε (DFS).
2. Perturbation magnitude growth: obtain a new ε (priority queue).

figures.description: Sequence of certified radii for different actions represented as a bar chart and state transitions.
Adaptive search algorithm visualization showing a tree structure for finding the minimum reward bound.

sections.title: Experiments
content: Setup:Four RL environments: Freeway, Pong, CartPole, Highway. Nine DQN variants including StdTrain, AdvTrain, etc.

sections.title: Robustness Certification for Per-State Action
content: GradDQN and RadialRL achieve the highest certified radius on Freeway.
Periodic patterns can be observed in Pong.

figures.description: Robustness certification results for per-state action across Freeway and Pong environments over time steps.

sections.title: Robustness Certification for Cumulative Reward
content: Under global smoothing, percentile bound is tighter than expectation bound.
The certification is relatively tight compared with the empirical result.

figures.description: Robustness certification results for cumulative reward showing global and local bounds against attack magnitude.
